---
title: "How to cite"
toc: false
---

::: {.hero}
# How to cite a Cornerstone Economics working paper
Each paper has a stable DOI from first release. Cite the DOI, not a URL.
:::

## Recommended format

Use the version DOI when citing a specific result, and the concept DOI when citing the paper as a body of work that may be revised. Both resolve at Zenodo. Example:

> de Kort, R. (2026). *Curaçao at the World Cup: what the FIFA ranking does not measure* (Cornerstone Economics Working Paper No. WP-2026-01). Cornerstone Economics. https://doi.org/10.5281/zenodo.XXXXXXX

## Machine-readable citations

Each paper repository ships a `CITATION.cff` file that GitHub renders into a "Cite this repository" widget and that reference managers can import directly. BibTeX, RIS, and CSL-JSON are also linked from each paper's entry on the [home page](index.qmd).

## Permanent archive

Papers are deposited at the [Cornerstone Economics Zenodo community](https://zenodo.org/communities/cornerstone-economics) on every tagged release. Zenodo provides preservation, DOI minting, and ORCID integration. The papers stay accessible even if Cornerstone Economics ever changes hosting or domain.
